title: Politicizing political liberalism: on the containment of illiberal and antidemocratic views [Book Review]
author: Fumagalli

opening_move.description: The review opens with the puzzle that Rawlsian scholarship has until recently largely ignored the challenge posed by antidemocratic forces, despite Rawls's own recognition that unreasonable doctrines are a permanent feature of democratic life.
opening_move.stakes_claimed: Political liberalism needs to reckon seriously with illiberal threats from within, not just treat them as marginal aberrations

theoretical_positioning.description: The review presents Badano and Nuti's 'politicization' of political liberalism as a necessary extension of Rawlsian theory to address antidemocratic threats, without independent critique.

epistemic_mode.interpretive_methods: close reading of Rawls and Badano/Nuti

case_type.case_name: Badano and Nuti, Politicizing Political Liberalism (2024); Rawls, Political Liberalism

normative_stake.domain: democratic theory / political liberalism / constitutional design

confidence_notes: Book review genre; primarily expository. The connection with militant democracy literature is implicit.
